name: list_entries
description: List content entries for a specific content type (collection type) in Strapi. Supports filtering by field values, sorting by one or more fields, pagination, field selection, and population of relations and components. The content type is specified as a pluralized name in the URL path (e.g., 'articles', 'products', 'categories'). Strapi uses a query parameter syntax with bracket notation for complex filters.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.description: The pluralized API name of the content type (e.g., 'articles', 'products', 'categories'). This is defined when creating the content type in the Strapi admin panel.

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description: Filter entries using Strapi's filter syntax. Use bracket notation in the query string: filters[field][$operator]=value. Operators include $eq, $ne, $lt, $gt, $lte, $gte, $in, $contains, $startsWith, $endsWith, $null, $notNull.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort by field and direction. Format: 'field:asc' or 'field:desc'. Multiple sorts: 'sort[0]=field1:asc&sort[1]=field2:desc'.

parameters.name: pagination[page]
parameters.description: Page number for pagination. Starts at 1. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: pagination[pageSize]
parameters.description: Number of entries per page. Maximum 100. Defaults to 25.

parameters.name: populate
parameters.description: Specify relations and components to populate. Use '*' to populate all first-level relations, or specify specific fields like 'populate[0]=author&populate[1]=categories'. Use 'populate=deep' with caution as it populates all nested levels.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Select specific fields to return. Format: 'fields[0]=title&fields[1]=slug'. Reduces response size by omitting unneeded fields.

attributes.title: Building REST APIs
attributes.content: Learn how to build robust REST APIs...
